front: What is calibration in IT Capacity Planning?
back: Calibration is the systematic process of adjusting and validating measurement tools, monitoring systems, and performance benchmarks to ensure they accurately reflect the true state of IT infrastructure.

front: What is the primary purpose of baseline calibration?
back: Baseline calibration establishes reference points for normal system performance, enabling accurate comparison against current metrics and anomaly detection.

front: What is metric drift in calibration context?
back: Metric drift is the gradual deviation of measured values from true values over time due to hardware degradation, software changes, or environmental factors.

front: Name two types of calibration in IT systems.
back: Hardware calibration (physical sensors, load balancers) and Software/Metric calibration (performance counters, monitoring tools).
Calibration interval is the time period between successive calibration activities, determined based on tool stability, usage frequency, and manufacturer recommendations.

front: What is validation in calibration process?
back: Validation is the verification step where calibrated tools are tested against known standards or reference values to confirm accuracy.
Traceable calibration links measurement results to national or international standards through an unbroken chain of comparisons, ensuring measurement reliability.
Accuracy is the degree to which a measured value conforms to the true value or accepted standard value.
Precision (or reproducibility) is the degree to which repeated measurements under unchanged conditions produce the same results.
A calibration factor is a correction value applied to raw measurements to adjust them to their true or standard values.
Zero calibration sets the baseline reading to zero when no load or input is present, eliminating systematic offset errors.
Span calibration adjusts the sensitivity or gain of a measurement system by setting readings at the upper end of the measurement range.

front: What is meant by calibration tolerance?
back: Calibration tolerance is the acceptable range of deviation from the standard value within which the instrument is considered calibrated.
End-to-end calibration tests the entire measurement path from data collection through processing to reporting, ensuring overall system accuracy.

front: Why is calibration critical for capacity planning?
back: Without proper calibration, capacity planning decisions based on inaccurate data can lead to over-provisioning (wasted resources) or under-provisioning (service failures).
